item.title: Why Is the Japanese Yen So Weak - and Will Authorities Intervene?
item.description: The yen’s weakness has become a growing concern for Japan’s policymakers, given its impact on import prices and household costs.

item.title: India Delays Stricter Trading Loan Rules as Volatility Climbs
item.description: India’s central bank has delayed the roll-out of stricter rules on loans to proprietary traders and some liquidity providers, offering relief as markets are roiled by volatility due to the Iran conflict.

item.title: Japan Spot Power Surges to Three-Year High as War Boosts Fuels
item.description: Spot power prices in Japan surged to a three-year high, as the war in the Middle East boosts fuel costs for economies across Asia.

item.title: Australia Aims to Use LNG Clout to Secure Asian Fuel Supplies
item.description: Australia is seeking to leverage its vast liquefied natural gas exports to Asia to secure fuel supplies in return, as the country grapples with shortages triggered by the war in Iran.

item.title: Malaysian Chemical Producer Tops Asia Stocks With 102% Rally
item.description: Shares of Malaysian petrochemical producer Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd. have doubled this month as fears of a prolonged Strait of Hormuz closure drive up global fertilizer prices, with analysts predicting more gains if the waterway remains shut.

item.title: Why Bond Markets Are Changing Their Mind on the War
item.description: The limits to growth are starting to weigh more heavily that the risk of inflation.

item.title: Kosovo FDI May Top 2025’s Record €1 Billion, Central Banker Says
item.description: Kosovo is on its way to attracting a record amount of foreign direct investment after last year’s level exceeded €1 billion ($1.2 billion) for the first time in the country’s history, according to the central bank governor.

item.title: South Korea Rolls Out $17 Billion Budget to Counter Iran Shock
item.description: South Korea unveiled a supplementary budget worth billions of dollars to cushion its economy from the fallout of the Iran war, as soaring oil prices and global supply disruptions intensify pressure on households and businesses.

item.title: Japan’s Two-Year Bond Auction Draws Demand on Elevated Yields
item.description: Japan’s two-year government bond auction passed smoothly as the high yield lured investors despite caution over a potential near-term rate hike by the Bank of Japan.

item.title: BOK Governor Pick Plays Down FX Risks, Spurns Dove-Hawk Labels
item.description: Bank of Korea governor nominee Hyun Song Shin played down the importance of recent slides in the local currency and cautioned against simplistic labeling of policy as dovish or hawkish as he prepares for a confirmation hearing.

item.title: US Senators Probe FCC Chief Over Fast-Tracked Nexstar-Tegna Deal
item.description: The ranking members of the Senate Commerce Committee questioned the head of the Federal Communications Commission in a Monday letter, criticizing his approval of Nexstar Media Group Inc.’s merger with Tegna Inc. without a vote of the full commission.

item.title: Philippine Inflation May Accelerate to a 20-Month High on Iran War, BSP Says
item.description: The raging war in Iran that caused domestic fuel prices to spike probably pushed this month’s inflation in import-dependent Philippines to the highest in nearly two years, according to the central bank’s forecast.

item.title: Aluminum Heads for 10% Monthly Surge as Iran War Roils Supplies
item.description: Aluminum headed for the biggest monthly gain in nearly two years, as the war in the Middle East disrupted supplies and damaged local production facilities, tightening the global market.

item.title: Thai PM Anutin Gets Cabinet Approval, Keeps Core Economic Team
item.description: Thailand’s new cabinet has secured royal approval, with Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul retaining his core economic team as the country grapples with rising fuel costs, weakening exports and slowing growth.

item.title: China’s State Oil and Gas Champions Tested by Turbulent Markets
item.description: China’s state-run oil and gas giants are holding back on aggressive expansion plans as they balance market turbulence against long‑term goals for energy security.

item.title: Moutai Shares Surge After It Hikes Price of Flagship Liquor
item.description: Shares of Kweichow Moutai Co. rose the most in about two months after China’s top liquor maker announced a price increase for its flagship Feitian Moutai spirit, in a move that could bolster its earnings prospects.

item.title: Malaysia Raises 2026 Growth Outlook, Sees War Impact Contained
item.description: Malaysia lifted its economic growth forecast for 2026, expecting strong domestic demand and investment to cushion the impact of the escalating war in the Middle East.

item.title: Oil Falls, Stocks Gain on Report Trump Mulling War Exit
Australia’s Fuel Tax Cut Will Take Time to Be Felt by Consumers
item.description: The impact of Australia’s move to halve tax on retail fuel prices will take time to filter through to consumers, Foreign Minister Penny Wong said in a televised interview.

item.title: JPMorgan Sees Australia IPO Demand Resilient to Stock Turmoil
item.description: Australia’s pipeline for initial public offerings is showing signs of resilience, even as the war in Iran rattles the stock market and fuels uncertainty, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co.

item.title: China Factory Activity Returns to Growth Despite War Fallout
item.description: China’s factory activity expanded for the first time this year despite higher energy prices and disruptions caused by the escalating conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Hedge Funds Chase Yen Strength Plays on Intervention Fears
item.description: Hedge fund demand for dollar-yen options that profit from a decline in the pair has increased after the currency moved past 160, amplifying intervention rhetoric from Japan’s Ministry of Finance.

item.title: Dollar Wraps Up Best Month Since 2022 Amid War in Middle East
item.description: The dollar is on track for its best month since September 2022 as war in the Middle East upends energy markets, buffets economic forecasts, and sends investors rushing to the world’s primary reserve currency.

item.title: High-Flying Chip Stocks Bear Brunt of Iran War Risk-Off Trade
item.description: As investors brace for a protracted conflict in the Middle East, they’re electing to sell the technology stocks that have been among the market’s biggest winners in recent months.

item.title: New Zealand Business Sentiment Hits Lowest Since 2024 Recession
item.description: New Zealand business sentiment slumped in March to the lowest since mid-2024 when the economy was in recession as the Middle East conflict hits the outlook for earnings and investment.

item.title: Mundys Plans to Boost Stake in Channel Tunnel Operator Getlink
item.description: Mundys SpA is close to unveiling a plan to increase its stake in Channel Tunnel operator Getlink SE, people with knowledge of the matter said, in a move aimed at deepening the Italian infrastructure group’s investments in France.

item.title: RBA Says Not Possible to Predict Cash Rate Path With Confidence
item.description: Australia’s central bank said it’s impossible to confidently predict the path for policy after raising interest rates for a second time this year as a surge in oil prices from the Middle East war fans inflation risks.

item.title: Asian Stocks Wipe Out 2026 Gains as War Drives Inflation Worries
item.description: Asian stocks fell, with the key regional benchmark erasing its gain for the year, as soaring energy costs due to the Iran war raise concerns of inflation and slower growth.

item.title: Activist Palliser Targets MSG Maker Ajinomoto in AI Hunt
item.description: UK-based activist fund Palliser Capital has expanded its hunt for overlooked AI beneficiaries in Japan with a stake in seasoning maker Ajinomoto Co., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Chinese Stocks Fare Better Than Global Peers in Iran-Driven Rout
item.description: Chinese stocks are emerging as one of the best markets to ride out the Iran war, with their outperformance against global peers on track for the strongest since August 2025.
